coverage.notes: Coverage tool does not instrument source-generator partial classes. Tests DO exercise the code (verified by tracing). Only Engine/this.cs (84.8%) appears in coverage.

findings.issue: Engine.Providers Get<T>(), Has<T>(), Remove<T>() have zero tests — 3 of 5 public methods on new infrastructure. Deletion test: these methods could be deleted without failing any test.
findings.impact: Every future module depends on Engine.Providers. A bug in Remove<T>() (e.g., wrong type key) or Has<T>() would go undetected. Provider hot-swapping at runtime would silently break.
findings.suggestion: Add a dedicated ProviderRegistryTests class testing all 5 public methods including register-remove-get round-trip

findings.issue: Hash_ObjectInput_SerializesToJsonBeforeHashing claims to verify JSON serialization but only checks consistency (same object hashed twice). If SerializeData() switched from JsonSerializer.Serialize() to data.ToString(), the test would still pass — but cross-system hash verification would silently break because input bytes changed.
findings.impact: Serialization boundary is the contract between systems. A change here would produce different hashes for the same logical data, breaking any stored/shared hashes. The test doesn't catch this.
findings.suggestion: Add a known-value test anchoring the exact bytes: hash of a specific object with keccak256 should equal a pre-computed hex string. This pins the serialization format.

findings.issue: Hash_ExplicitAlgorithm_OverridesDefault checks algorithm name and hash length but not that the hash value differs from keccak256. Both algorithms produce 32 bytes (64 hex chars). If the Algorithm property were ignored in hash.cs, this test would still pass.
findings.impact: DefaultProviderTests verify each algorithm in isolation, but this handler test is supposed to verify that the Algorithm parameter flows through the handler correctly. It doesn't — the integration gap means a hardcoded algorithm in hash.cs would go undetected at the handler level.
findings.suggestion: Hash same input with keccak256 and sha256 via the handler, assert the hashes differ

findings.issue: 6 PLang .goal tests exist but cannot be built or run — crypto module not registered with builder yet
findings.impact: Full pipeline validation (goal → builder → .pr → runtime) deferred until builder integration (piece 8)
findings.suggestion: Build and run PLang tests after crypto module is registered with the builder

findings.impact: Minimal — convenience method returning Hash property

summary: Test suite structure is solid but has 3 major findings: Engine.Providers has 60% untested public API (deletion test fails), the JSON serialization test is a false green (doesn't prove JSON happened), and the algorithm override test doesn't verify the hash actually changed. These mean subtle bugs in provider lifecycle, serialization format, or algorithm routing would go undetected. Provider error propagation tests (review-driven) are strong. PLang tests deferred (known).
